MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Oct-05-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT SUTTER BAY HOSPITALS' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication is GRANTED. Sutter submitted substantial admissible evidence showing that it had a legitimate nondiscriminatory reason for its termination of the employment of plaintiff Marcial Miranda. Sutter showed that Mr. Miranda repeatedly violated Sutter's policies, received multiple warnings that his next violation may result in termination, and his employment was terminated after he violated the "48 hour rule" of the food storage and preparation policy. Mr. Miranda failed to submit substantial admissible evidence that Sutter's proffered nondiscriminatory reason for his employment termination was a pretext for intentional discrimination. Mr. Miranda's assertion that Ms. Infante had animosity against Filipinos and Chinese workers is speculative and unsupported by any specific admissible evidence. Mr. Miranda's assertion that the "48 hour rule" was not strictly followed is also unsupported by evidence. The evidence shows that Mr. Miranda was aware of the "48 hour rule" and, even if supervisors in the past had instructed him to extend the food expiration date, in the instance that led to his termination he did not receive permission to violate that rule. Written order is signed and filed in open court.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Not reported. (L&M302)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Bifurcate Punitive Damages From The Liability Phase Of Trial And To Exclude Evidence Of Or Reference To Defendants Financial Condition
Off Calendar Oct-05-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances.

The Court confirms the tentative ruling.

DEFENDANT SUTTER BAY HOSPITALS' Motion To Bifurcate Punitive Damages From The Liability Phase Of Trial And To Exclude Evidence Of Or Reference To Defendants Financial Condition is OFF CALENDAR in light of the ruling on Sutter Bay's summary judgment.

Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: J. Rios Merida; Reporter: Not reported. (L&M302)
